post.description:
Google OAuth2 sign-in via ID token from the frontend.

Request Body:
    id_token (str): Google ID token from frontend OAuth2 flow.
    role     (str): 'vendor' or 'client' (for new registrations).

Success 201 (NEW USER):
    { "status": "success", "message": "Welcome...", "is_new": true, "tokens": {...}, "redirect": "/dashboard?welcome=true" }

Success 200 (EXISTING USER):
    { "status": "success", "message": "Welcome back...", "is_new": false, "tokens": {...}, "redirect": "/dashboard" }

Error 401 — Invalid or expired Google token.
Error 500 — Unexpected server error.

get.summary: Return the last 10 login events (attempts) for the authenticated user.
get.description:
Useful for the Security Dashboard "Recent Login Activity" section.
Analogous to Binance's "Login Activity" and Google's "Recent Security Events".

Uses LoginEventSerializer for validated, Swagger-documented responses.
Uses get_login_events() selector for optimized ORM query with select_related.

post.description:
SyncAuthService.login() performs (in order):
  1. Soft-delete pre-check → SoftDeletedUserError (403)
  2. Django authenticate() → password verification
  3. is_verified check    → AccountNotVerifiedError (403) with OTP URLs
  4. is_active check      → AccountDeactivatedError (403) with support URL
  5. Invalid credentials  → InvalidCredentialsError (401)
  6. LoginEvent INSERT (every attempt — success AND failure)
  7. AuditService.log()  (async via Celery on_commit)
  8. UserSession.create_from_token() via transaction.on_commit()
  9. lifecycle_counter Celery task (fire-and-forget)

This provides a complete, tamper-proof audit trail for every login
attempt — critical for SIEM dashboards and compliance requirements.
Blacklists refresh token, invalidating the server-side session.

get.description: Return the requesting user's profile via MeSerializer.

post.description:
Synchronous User Registration.

Accepts email OR phone + password + role, creates the user inside an
atomic transaction, generates a 6-digit OTP, and dispatches it via
Email (if email provided) or SMS (if phone only).

Mirrors legacy:  ``userauths.views.RegisterViewCelery``

Request Body:
    email     (str, optional)  : User email address.
    phone     (str, optional)  : Phone in E.164 format (+234...).
    password  (str, required)  : Min 8 chars, must contain upper/lower/digit/special.
    password2 (str, required)  : Must match password.
    role      (str, required)  : 'vendor' or 'client'.

Success Response 201:
    {
      "message": "Registration successful. Check your email/phone for OTP.",
      "user_id": "<uuid>",
      "email":   "user@example.com",
      "phone":   null
    }

Error Responses:
    400 — Validation error (missing fields, weak password, mismatch, duplicate)
    500 — Unexpected server error (transaction rolled back)
Resends OTP to user's verified contact.

get.summary: List all active sessions for the authenticated user.
get.description:
Returns the 20 most recent sessions, sorted by last_used_at descending.
Each session includes device, location, and is_current flag.

The ``is_current`` field marks the session being used for this request,
allowing the frontend to highlight it (styled differently, no revoke button).

post.summary: Logout all other devices — keeps only the current session.
post.description:
Useful when a user suspects their account is compromised on another device.
All other sessions except the current request's session are blacklisted
and deleted in a single atomic transaction.

Returns the count of sessions terminated.

delete.summary: Revoke a session by its UUID7 primary key (string).
delete.description:
All models inherit from CommonTimestampModel which uses UUID7 as PK.
The URL parameter is <str:session_id> — NOT <int:session_id>.

post.description: Delegates to SimpleJWT TokenRefreshView with custom renderer.
Verifies OTP (OTP-only, no user_id in request) and issues JWT tokens.

get.description:
Return all paid orders for the authenticated client.
Prefetches orderitems to avoid N+1 on order detail pages.

Return detail for a single paid order scoped to this client.
Uses oid (order identifier) — not PK — for public safety.

post.summary: Authenticated user changes their own password from the dashboard.
post.description:
Permission gate (strictest possible for account-mutating actions):
  IsVerifiedUser  — user must be authenticated, active, AND OTP-verified.

The ``@require_verification`` decorator adds an inline double-check so
that even if permission_classes is accidentally overridden in a subclass,
the verification check still runs inside the method body.

URL parameters:
  uidb64  — base64-encoded user PK (from the magic link).
  token   — HMAC one-time-use Django password-reset token.
Finalise a phone-based password reset via OTP.

post.summary: Initiate a password reset — email or phone.
post.description:
Accepts ``{ "email_or_phone": "..." }`` and dispatches either a
magic-link email or an SMS OTP, depending on the identifier type.

Always returns the same generic message (anti-enumeration).
Rate-limited: 100 req/hour per IP.

AuditService event: PASSWORD_RESET_REQUESTED (compliance=True)

get.description:
GET  /api/v1/profile/me/ — Return authenticated user's full profile.
PATCH /api/v1/profile/me/ — Partial update of authenticated user profile.

GET  uses: UserProfileSerializer (all readable fields)
PATCH uses: update_user_profile() service (guarded, validated writes)

patch.description:
GET  /api/v1/profile/me/ — Return authenticated user's full profile.
PATCH /api/v1/profile/me/ — Partial update of authenticated user profile.

GET  uses: UserProfileSerializer (all readable fields)
PATCH uses: update_user_profile() service (guarded, validated writes)

get.summary: GET /api/v1/profile/users/ — Admin-only list of all registered users.
get.description: Returns ProtectedUserSerializer shape — no sensitive fields exposed.

post.description:
Generate a time-limited, HMAC-SHA256–signed Cloudinary upload token.
The frontend uses this to POST a file DIRECTLY to Cloudinary without
routing the upload data through the Django server.

get.description:
Full analytics snapshot: today's sales, this month's sales, YTD,
average order value, total customers, low stock count.
Uses model analytics methods (reverse related_name ORM — no N+1).

Top 5 performing categories by revenue.
Uses vendor_products → category__name / order_item_product__total.

Customer behaviour: hourly order distribution + new customers this month.
Uses vendor_orders reverse FK.

Revenue distribution by payment_status as percentages.
Uses vendor_orders reverse FK.

Monthly count of orders grouped by order_status for chart rendering.
Uses vendor_orders reverse FK.

Monthly count of products created over the last 12 months.
Uses vendor_products reverse FK.

Monthly revenue breakdown for the last N months.
Uses vendor.get_revenue_trends() — reverse vendor_orders FK.

Vendor's coupons with optional active/inactive filter.
Uses vendor_coupons reverse FK.

Comprehensive earning tracker:
today / this month / last month / year / total.
Also returns pending_payouts (unpaid orders total).

Vendor's own orders, with optional filtering by payment_status / order_status.
Uses vendor_orders reverse FK.
Count of orders grouped by payment_status for dashboard badges.

Single order detail for this vendor.
Uses vendor_orders reverse FK to scope the query.

patch.description:
Update order_status for a vendor's own order.
Allowed values: Pending, Processing, Shipped, Fulfilled, Cancelled.
Payment status (paid/unpaid) is immutable here — managed by payment gateway.

post.description: POST /api/v1/vendor/payout/ — save bank / payout account details
POST /api/v1/vendor/pin/set/ — set 4-digit payout confirmation PIN
POST /api/v1/vendor/pin/verify/ — verify payout PIN before withdrawal

get.description:
Vendor's own product list with optional title/status filtering.
Uses vendor_products reverse FK.

post.description:
Create a new product scoped to this vendor.
Accepts multipart/form-data with optional nested fields:
  specifications[0][title], specifications[0][content]
  colors[0][name], colors[0][color_code], colors[0][image]
  sizes[0][name], sizes[0][price]
  gallery[0][image]

get.description:
GET /api/v1/vendor/products/filter/?status=published|draft|disabled|in-review
GET /api/v1/vendor/products/filter/?ordering=latest|oldest

Filter + sort vendor's own products using query params.
All status values: published, draft, disabled, in-review.
Ordering values: latest (default), oldest.

get.description:
Products with stock quantity below the given threshold.
Uses vendor_products reverse FK.

Top products by total quantity sold.
Uses vendor_products → order_item_product__qty.

delete.description:
Hard-delete a vendor's own product.
Scoped via vendor_products reverse FK — cannot delete another vendor's product.

put.description:
PUT  /api/v1/vendor/products/<product_pid>/edit/  — full update
PATCH /api/v1/vendor/products/<product_pid>/edit/ — partial update

Scoped to this vendor via vendor_products reverse FK.
Replaces all nested specs/colors/sizes/gallery on update.

member_id.title: Member id
member_id.description: Unique human-readable brand ID (e.g. FASTAR000062). Auto-generated on user creation. Cannot be changed.

email.title: Email address
email.description: Primary unique identifier for email-based auth.

role.title: Role
role.description: RBAC Role. Cannot be changed after creation.

is_verified.title: Is verified
is_verified.description: True if email/phone OTP is verified.

is_active.title: Is active
is_active.description: True if user account is active.

is_staff.title: Staff status
is_staff.description: Designates whether the user can log into this admin site.

email_or_phone.title: Email or phone
email_or_phone.description: User's email or phone for resend OTP

refresh.title: Refresh
refresh.description: A valid refresh token previously issued by the login endpoint.
